_comment: English language templates for semantic didactic layer.

ionic.rule.question: Rule: Δχ ≥ {value} or {ref:concept:metals} + {ref:concept:nonmetals} with high {ref:concept:electronegativity} difference

ionic.description.question:
Bond between a {ref:concept:metals} and a {ref:concept:nonmetals} by {ref:concept:electron_transfer}. Ions are formed: {ref:concept:cation} (+) and {ref:concept:anion} (−).
Properties: High melting points, brittle, conducts electricity in melt and solution

covalent_polar.description.question:
Bond between different {ref:concept:nonmetals} via {ref:concept:shared_electron_pair}. {ref:concept:electron_density} is shifted toward the more electronegative atom ({ref:concept:partial_charge}: δ+, δ−).
Properties: Various m.p., often liquids or gases, does not conduct electricity

covalent_nonpolar.description.question:
Bond between identical {ref:concept:nonmetals} via {ref:concept:shared_electron_pair}. {ref:concept:electron_density} is distributed symmetrically.
Properties: Low m.p., gases or low-boiling liquids, does not conduct electricity

metallic.description.question:
Bond in {ref:concept:metals}: atoms release valence electrons into a shared "electron cloud" ({ref:concept:delocalized_electron}) that holds positive ions in the lattice.
Properties: Electrical and thermal conductivity, malleability, {ref:concept:metallic_luster}

lattice_comparison.row.concept:ionic_cry.question: {ref:concept:ionic_crystal_lattice|short}|{ref:concept:cation} and {ref:concept:anion}|High (800–3000 °C)|Hard, brittle|In melt and solution|Often soluble in water|NaCl, KBr, CaO

lattice_comparison.row.concept:atomic_cr.question: {ref:concept:atomic_crystal_lattice|short}|Atoms (cov. bond)|Very high (> 1500 °C)|Very hard|Does not conduct (except graphite)|Insoluble|C (diamond), Si, SiO₂

lattice_comparison.row.concept:metallic_.question: {ref:concept:metallic_crystal_lattice|short}|Metal ions + electron gas|Variable (−39 °C for Hg to 3422 °C for W)|Variable (Na soft, W hard)|High|Insoluble|Fe, Cu, Al, Au

oxidizer_def.text.question: An {ref:prop:oxidizer} is the atom that accepts electrons. Its {ref:prop:oxidation_state} decreases ({ref:concept:reduction}).

reducer_def.text.question: A {ref:prop:reducer} is the atom that donates electrons. Its {ref:prop:oxidation_state} increases ({ref:concept:oxidation}).

mnemonic.text.question: OIL RIG: {ref:prop:oxidizer} Is {ref:concept:reduction} (accepts e⁻, OS↓). {ref:prop:reducer} Is {ref:concept:oxidation} (donates e⁻, OS↑).
